title: silene ammophila subsp. ammophila
intro: Silene ammophila subsp. ammophila, also known as sand catchfly, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Silene ammophila subsp. ammophila include its potential to treat various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Silene ammophila subsp. ammophila can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
